factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has increased compared to the prior year, primarily due to favorable working capital fluctuations.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased $165 million for the six months ended July 3, 2016, versus the comparable period in 2015, primarily due to favorable working capital fluctuations and an increase in deferred income taxes, partially offset by lower consolidated net income, decreases from translation and hedging activities and restructuring payments.

factors.summary: The company is increasing shareholder returns through common stock repurchases and dividend payments.

evidence.text: In the first six months of 2016, we made the following purchases under the respective stock repurchase programs: ... We paid dividends of $333 million during the six months ended July 3, 2016.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is increasing, primarily due to higher common stock repurchases and dividend payments.

evidence.text: The increase in our debt to capital ratio was due to the repurchases of common stock and higher total debt, primarily due to the commercial paper program.
evidence.subsection: Key Working Capital and Balance Sheet Data

factors.summary: The company is facing challenges in talent retention, as evidenced by workforce reductions and restructuring actions.

evidence.text: We reduced our worldwide workforce by approximately 1,900 employees. We incurred a fourth quarter charge of $90 million ( $61 million after tax) for these headcount reductions...

factors.summary: The company is facing pricing pressure, particularly in the Distribution segment, due to higher selling, general, and administrative expenses.

evidence.text: Distribution segment EBIT for the three months ended July 3, 2016, decrease d $26 million versus the comparable period in 2015, primarily due to higher selling, general and administrative expenses (mainly related to the acquisition of North American distributors since December 31, 2014) and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in the Nigerian naira), partially offset by higher gross margin.

factors.summary: Unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations are impacting multiple segments and regions, leading to revenue and margin pressures.

evidence.text: Components segment sales for the three months ended July 3, 2016, decrease d $118 million across all lines of business versus the comparable period in 2015. The following were the primary drivers: •Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales results primarily in the Chinese renminbi, Brazilian real, and British pound.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable with decreases due to restructuring actions.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses decreased $13 million for the three months ended July 3, 2016, versus the comparable period in 2015, primarily due to lower compensation expenses as a result of restructuring actions in 2015 and lower consulting expenses.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through restructuring and efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: Research, development and engineering expenses decreased $11 million... primarily due to lower compensation expenses as a result of restructuring actions in 2015, lower consulting expenses and increased expense recovery from customers and external parties.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction includes focusing on new product development to meet future emission standards.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world and improvements in fuel economy performance.

factors.summary: New product launches and improved margins from the acquisition of North American distributors are catalysts for improvement.

evidence.text: Partially offset by increased Distribution segment sales of 3 percent, primarily due to higher sales related to the acquisition of North American distributors since December 31, 2014.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are creating value through increased sales and margins.

evidence.text: Partially offset by increased Distribution segment sales of 3 percent, primarily due to higher sales related to the acquisition of North American distributors since December 31, 2014.

factors.summary: R&D investment is focused on new product development, which is on track.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world and improvements in fuel economy performance.

factors.summary: New products are under development to meet future emission standards, which is a positive sign.

evidence.text: Research activities continue to focus on development of new products to meet future emission standards around the world and improvements in fuel economy performance.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations are creating compliance costs, particularly related to emission standards.

evidence.text: Unpredictability in the adoption, implementation and enforcement of emission standards around the world.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2.   Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations / CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to expected challenges in key markets and ongoing risks.

evidence.text: Our outlook reflects the following challenges to our business that may reduce our revenue and earnings potential for the remainder of 2016: We expect industry production of heavy-duty trucks in North America to decline. We expect power generation markets to remain weak. We believe weak economic conditions in Brazil will continue to negatively impact demand across our businesses. Foreign currency volatility could continue to put pressure on our revenues and earnings. We expect market demand to remain weak in the oil and gas markets as the result of low crude oil prices. We expect demand for equipment in global mining markets to remain weak.

factors.summary: Restructuring actions have led to significant cost savings, particularly in labor expenses, resulting in lower selling, general, and administrative expenses, and are expected to deliver annualized savings of approximately $160 million.

evidence.text: Restructuring actions have led to significant cost savings, particularly in labor expenses.

We executed restructuring actions primarily in the form of professional voluntary and involuntary employee separation programs in the fourth quarter of 2015. We reduced our worldwide workforce by approximately 1,900 employees. We incurred a fourth quarter charge of $90 million ( $61 million after tax) for these headcount reductions, of which $86 million was expected to be settled in cash.
The decrease in selling, general and administrative expenses was primarily due to lower compensation expenses as the result of restructuring actions taken in December 2015.
Selling, general and administrative expenses decreased $13 million... primarily due to lower compensation expenses as a result of restructuring actions in 2015 and lower consulting expenses.
We expect to realize annualized savings from the 2015 restructuring actions of approximately $160 million.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing a negative margin trajectory, with declining gross margins across multiple segments due to lower volumes, unfavorable pricing, and unfavorable mix. While selling, general, and administrative expenses have decreased in some areas, particularly due to restructuring actions, other segments are seeing higher selling, general, and administrative expenses, contributing to the compression of operating margins. Additionally, foreign currency fluctuations have negatively impacted margins, particularly in regions like Brazil and China.

evidence.text: The decrease in gross margin for the three months ended July 3, 2016, versus the comparable period in 2015, was primarily due to lower volumes and unfavorable pricing.

Components segment EBIT for the three months ended July 3, 2016, decreased $33 million versus the comparable period in 2015, primarily due to lower gross margin and higher selling, general and administrative expenses, partially offset by lower research, development and engineering expenses.

Gross margin decreased 0.2 points as a percentage of sales... The decrease in gross margin dollars was primarily due to lower volumes and unfavorable mix, partially offset by lower material and commodity costs.
Gross margin decreased by 10% in the three months ended July 3, 2016, compared to the same period in 2015, primarily due to lower volumes and unfavorable mix.

factors.summary: Volume trends are negative, with declines across multiple segments, regions, and key markets, particularly in on-highway and industrial sectors. While light-duty engine shipments saw a slight increase, the overall trend reflects lower demand and unfavorable factors such as foreign currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: The decrease in gross margin for the three months ended July 3, 2016, versus the comparable period in 2015, was primarily due to lower volumes and unfavorable pricing.

Components segment sales for the three months ended July 3, 2016, decrease d $118 million across all lines of business versus the comparable period in 2015. The following were the primary drivers: •Emission solutions sales decrease d $55 million primarily due to lower demand in North American on-highway markets, partially offset by higher demand in Western Europe and China. •Turbo technologies sales decrease d $31 million primarily due to lower demand in North American on-highway markets. •Fuel systems sales decrease d $28 million primarily due to lower demand in the North American on-highway markets. •Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales results primarily in the Chinese renminbi, Brazilian real, and British pound.
Unit shipments by engine classification... Heavy-duty: 20,700 vs. 32,800, a decrease of 37%. Mid-range: 62,300 vs. 66,600, a decrease of 6%. Light-duty: 57,100 vs. 53,400, an increase of 7%

Worldwide revenues decreased 10 percent in the three months ended July 3, 2016, as compared to the same period in 2015, primarily due to lower demand in most global on-highway markets, unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations and decreased demand in most global power generation markets, partially offset by sales increases related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2014.

factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in key markets such as North American on-highway and off-highway sectors, with a notable impact on global on-highway markets.

evidence.text: Net sales for the three months ended July 3, 2016, decreased by $487 million versus the comparable period in 2015. The primary drivers were lower demand in North American on-highway markets and lower demand in all North American off-highway markets.

Worldwide revenues decreased 10 percent in the three months ended July 3, 2016, as compared to the same period in 2015, primarily due to lower demand in most global on-highway markets, unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations and decreased demand in most global power generation markets, partially offset by sales increases related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2014.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, negatively impacting profitability. While the Distribution segment experienced a 3% increase in sales due to acquisitions and higher demand, this shift is offset by declines in international and on-highway markets, driven by unfavorable foreign currency impacts and lower demand in key regions.

evidence.text: The decreases above were partially offset by increased Distribution segment sales of 3 percent, primarily due to higher sales related to the acquisition of North American distributors since December 31, 2014, partially offset by a decline in organic sales in North American oil and gas markets.

The decline in international sales was primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency impacts of 1 percent (primarily in the Chinese renminbi, Brazilian real, Indian rupee, Australian dollar and British pound) and lower demand in the on-highway markets, especially in Mexico.

The decline in international sales was primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency impacts of 2 percent (primarily in the Brazilian real, Chinese renminbi, Indian rupee, Australian dollar, British pound and South African rand), lower demand in the on-highway markets in Brazil and Mexico and decreased demand in international industrial markets led by declines in marine and mining markets.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with slowing demand in North American and global off-highway markets, as well as weakness in power generation and oil and gas sectors, offset somewhat by strong demand for North American pick-up trucks.

evidence.text: The primary drivers were lower demand in North American on-highway markets and lower demand in all North American off-highway markets.

We expect demand for pick-up trucks in North America to remain strong. We expect power generation markets to remain weak. We expect market demand to remain weak in the oil and gas markets as the result of low crude oil prices.

factors.summary: Input costs have shown a positive trend with declining material and commodity costs, providing relief. This decline has been consistent across different sections of the filing.

evidence.text: Lower material and commodity costs, lower warranty expense and increased Distribution margins related to the acquisition of North American distributors since December 31, 2014.

The decrease in gross margin was primarily due to lower volumes and unfavorable mix, partially offset by lower material and commodity costs, lower warranty expense and increased Distribution margins related to the acquisition of North American distributors since December 31, 2014.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks primarily related to foreign currency fluctuations, particularly in emerging markets, weak demand in power generation and oil and gas markets, and economic conditions in Brazil. Additionally, there are concerns about foreign currency volatility impacting revenues and earnings.

evidence.text: Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales by approximately 2 percent primarily in the Chinese renminbi, Brazilian real, Indian rupee, Australian dollar, and British pound.

We expect power generation markets to remain weak. We expect market demand to remain weak in the oil and gas markets as the result of low crude oil prices. We believe weak economic conditions in Brazil will continue to negatively impact demand across our businesses. Foreign currency volatility could continue to put pressure on our revenues and earnings.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to declining demand and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: Distribution segment sales for the three months ended July 3, 2016, increase d $49 million versus the comparable period in 2015, primarily due to $114 million of segment sales related to the acquisition of North American distributors since December 31, 2014, partially offset by a decline in organic sales of $41 million (primarily due to North American oil and gas markets) and unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations (primarily in the Australian dollar, Canadian dollar, and South African rand).

Net sales for the three months ended July 3, 2016, decreased by $487 million versus the comparable period in 2015. The primary drivers were lower demand in North American on-highway markets and lower demand in all North American off-highway markets.

Worldwide revenues decreased 10 percent in the three months ended July 3, 2016, as compared to the same period in 2015, primarily due to lower demand in most global on-highway markets, unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations and decreased demand in most global power generation markets, partially offset by sales increases related to the consolidation of partially-owned North American distributors since December 31, 2014.

factors.summary: The company faces significant risks from foreign currency fluctuations, particularly affecting international sales and impacting regions like Brazil, China, India, Australia, Canada, the UK, and South Africa. Additionally, demand in on-highway and industrial markets, especially in Brazil and Mexico, and broader economic conditions in Brazil are key sensitivities that could affect near-term results negatively.

evidence.text: Unfavorable foreign currency fluctuations... primarily in the Brazilian real, Chinese renminbi, Indian rupee, Australian dollar, Canadian dollar, British pound and South African rand.

The decline in international sales was primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency impacts of 2 percent (primarily in the Brazilian real, Chinese renminbi, Indian rupee, Australian dollar, British pound and South African rand), lower demand in the on-highway markets in Brazil and Mexico and decreased demand in international industrial markets led by declines in marine and mining markets.

factors.summary: The evidence presents a mixed picture regarding construction and infrastructure spending. On one hand, there is an increase in construction activity in international markets, which is supporting demand. On the other hand, the broader economic environment, particularly in the second quarter of 2016, showed a decline in demand due to global economic weakness, leading to a decrease in international revenues and sales in several key markets.

evidence.text: Increased unit shipments of 37 percent in international construction markets.

Continued global economic weakness in the second quarter of 2016 negatively impacted our international revenues (excludes the U.S. and Canada), which declined by 4 percent, with sales down in many of our markets, especially in the U.K., Mexico and Brazil. The decline in international sales was primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency impacts of 2 percent (primarily in the Brazilian real, Chinese renminbi, Indian rupee, Australian dollar, British pound and South African rand), lower demand in the on-highway markets in Brazil and Mexico and decreased demand in international industrial markets led by declines in marine and mining markets.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are negatively impacting the company's results, affecting both sales and earnings. The impact is primarily due to unfavorable fluctuations in several emerging currencies, including the Chinese renminbi, Brazilian real, Indian rupee, Australian dollar, British pound, and South African rand.

evidence.text: Foreign currency fluctuations unfavorably impacted sales by approximately 2 percent primarily in the Chinese renminbi, Brazilian real, Indian rupee, Australian dollar, and British pound.

The decline in international sales was primarily due to unfavorable foreign currency impacts of 2 percent (primarily in the Brazilian real, Chinese renminbi, Indian rupee, Australian dollar, British pound, and South African rand), lower demand in the on-highway markets in Brazil and Mexico, and decreased demand in international industrial markets led by declines in marine and mining markets.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a significant headwind, impacting both emerging markets and broader global demand. The challenges include lower demand across product lines and regions, particularly in areas like China, North America, Asia (excluding China), the Middle East, the U.K., Mexico, and Brazil, as well as weakness in the oil and gas markets due to low crude oil prices.

evidence.text: Lower demand in all product lines and decreased sales in most regions with the largest declines in China, North America, Asia (excluding China) and the Middle East.

Continued global economic weakness in the second quarter of 2016 negatively impacted our international revenues (excludes the U.S. and Canada), which declined by 4 percent, with sales down in many of our markets, especially in the U.K., Mexico and Brazil. We expect market demand to remain weak in the oil and gas markets as the result of low crude oil prices.
